reasoning: The fetch_ts_yfinance tool returned an incorrect time series (prices in the $1800-$2200 range instead of SO's actual $95 range), so its statistical output was discarded. Relying on base rates and recent fundamentals, recent Q2 2025 earnings reported on July 31 showed an adjusted EPS miss ($0.92 vs $0.93 expected) and a GAAP net income decline. This typically injects modest downward pressure or post-earnings drag over the succeeding weeks. Furthermore, Southern Company usually pays its Q3 dividend mid-August, meaning the stock will likely go ex-dividend prior to the September 2 resolution date, creating a structural drag on the share price of approximately $0.72. Thus, probabilities for August and September are positioned slightly below 0.50. For November and January, the natural equity risk premium and multi-month positive drift help overcome the dividend payouts. Abiding by the mandate that probabilities for distant dates should converge closer to 0.5, 3-month and 6-month probabilities are held at a very symmetric 0.51.
